---
title: "ssh from ubuntu 7.1 not working"
date: 2008-05-06
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by vidyadhara on 2008-05-06
I am trying to ssh from my ubuntu 7.1 installation to a fedora 5 installation.

I type in 

ssh <username>@192.168.1.20

and wait forever

about 10 minutes later the command prompt comes back and says 

ssh: connect to host 192.168.1.20 port 22: Connection timed out

The firewall on the remote host is definitely allowing ssh connections to come through because I am able to connect from a fedora 6 machine to the same host.

The ubuntu machine I am doing this from is allowing incoming ssh connections from the remote machine.

---

### Post by glennric on 2008-05-06
Is your Ubuntu computer on the same network as the other Fedora computers?

---

### Post by njparton on 2008-05-06
Have you changed any iptables settings in ubuntu?

---

### Post by Monicker on 2008-05-06
Sounds like it could be a basic connectivity issue.

Can you ping the fedora 5 machine from the Ubuntu one?  If the Ubuntu machine is using a static ip, check and make sure it has the proper subnet mask.

If you run tcpdump on the Fedora 5 machine, does it even show connection attempts from the Ubuntu machine?

As root on the Fedora box:
```
tcpdump port 22 and host <ubuntu ip address> and host <fedora ip address>
```

---

### Post by bodhi.zazen on 2008-05-06
try :

ssh -vv ssh <username>@192.168.1.20

the additional output is often helpful in de bugging.

I am still waiting for the command to terminate on the ubuntu machine

Hrmm. Okay.  There is traffic from Ubuntu to Fedora in the tcpdump you have shown, but I do not see traffic from Fedora to Ubuntu.  Ubuntu is not getting any replies back from Fedora.  Doing any outbound firewalling on the Fedora side?  Also check /var/log/messages and /var/log/auth.log on the Fedora side.

This is different from what you showed earlier in tcpdump.  There is back and forth traffic between both boxes, and the Fedora side is replying.

I would run the same tcpdump command on the Ubuntu box and try the connection again.  If you are still getting timeouts in Ubuntu, I am inclined to think its a firewall issue on the Ubuntu side.

Did you find anything in the Fedora logs?

### Post by Monicker on 2008-05-06
I think we are at an impasse without some useful ssh log information from both sides.


Do ssh -vvv username@192.168.1.20 from Ubuntu again, and show all output to the point of the connection timing out.


Grep for ssh in the logs on the Fedora side.


EDIT:  That grep from the Ubuntu side shows iptables log entries.

Run this as root on both machines, and then try again:

```
iptables -F
```

---

### Post by vidyadhara on 2008-05-06
> **Monicker said:**
> I think we are at an impasse without some useful ssh log information from both sides.


Do ssh -vvv username@192.168.1.20 from Ubuntu again, and show all output to the point of the connection timing out.


Grep for ssh in the logs on the Fedora side.


EDIT:  That grep from the Ubuntu side shows iptables log entries.

Run this as root on both machines, and then try again:

```
iptables -F
```
